stages.d8: Scale successful pilots and adopt them as standard practice.

terms.title: Ownership & IP Terms
terms.subtitle: Terms and conditions governing ownership of ideas submitted to the Innovation Platform of the General Authority for Competition.

sections.heading: 1. Definitions
sections.body: "Idea" means any proposal, suggestion, innovation or solution submitted by a participant through the GAC Innovation Platform, whether written, visual, or accompanied by supporting documents. "Intellectual Property" includes any patent rights, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, industrial designs, and any related rights arising from the idea.

sections.heading: 2. Ownership of the Idea
sections.body: Upon submission of an idea through the platform, the participant acknowledges that all intellectual property rights related to the idea transfer to the General Authority for Competition. GAC has the exclusive right to develop, implement, modify, and use the idea for any purpose, including licensing to or assignment to third parties.

sections.heading: 3. Originality Warranty
sections.body: The participant warrants that the submitted idea is their original work and that it does not infringe upon any third-party intellectual property rights. The participant bears full responsibility for any legal claims arising from allegations of infringement.

sections.heading: 4. Confidentiality
sections.body: GAC is committed to maintaining the confidentiality of submitted ideas according to the level of confidentiality designated by the participant (public, internal, or confidential). Confidential ideas are accessed only by the authorized evaluation team, solely for screening and evaluation purposes.

sections.heading: 5. Attribution
sections.body: GAC acknowledges the participant as the original author of the idea and may credit them in implementation documents and related publications, at its discretion.

sections.heading: 6. Incentives and Rewards
sections.body: GAC may, at its sole discretion, grant the participant a moral or material reward if their idea is adopted and implemented. Submission of an idea does not in itself entitle the participant to any reward; this is governed by GAC's approved incentive policy.

sections.heading: 7. Ownership Exceptions
sections.body: The ownership transfer terms do not apply to ideas submitted by external rights holders under pre-existing partnership or licensing agreements, or ideas submitted by other government entities subject to their own regulations. In such cases, ownership is determined per written agreement.

sections.heading: 8. Agreement
sections.body: By submitting your idea through the platform and agreeing to these terms, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to all the terms above, and that you waive any right to claim ownership of the idea after submission as specified.

sections.heading: 9. Governing Law
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, particularly the Copyright Law, the Patents and Industrial Designs Law, the Trademarks Law, and the Competition Law. The competent courts in Riyadh have jurisdiction over any dispute arising from the application of these terms.

terms.agreementNote: By clicking "I agree" on the idea submission page, you acknowledge that you have read and agreed to all the terms above.

about.title: About the Program
about.subtitle: Innovation to Impact — the innovation journey of the General Authority for Competition.

hero.headline: Turning Ideas into Impact
hero.subheadline: GAC's platform for structured innovation across the organization.

vision.label: Our Vision
vision.body: To build a culture of innovation across the General Authority for Competition — where every employee can shape a fairer, more competitive Saudi market aligned with Vision 2030.

mission.label: Our Mission
mission.body: To capture ideas from across the organization and guide them through a transparent, measurable pipeline — from strategic framing to real, tracked impact.

pipeline.title: The 9-Stage Pipeline
pipeline.body: Every idea moves through a structured journey — from intake and strategic framing, through evaluation and committee review, into pilot, implementation, and measured benefits. Each stage has clear owners, criteria, and expected outputs so nothing gets lost between an idea and its impact.

participants.title: Who Can Participate
participants.subtitle: The platform is open to every part of the Authority.

items.title: GAC employees at all levels
items.body: From frontline staff to leadership — anyone with an idea that can improve how the Authority delivers on its mandate.

items.title: All departments and functions
items.body: Legal, economic, market monitoring, technology, communications, HR, and every other function across the Authority.

items.title: Individual contributors and teams
items.body: Submit on your own or bring an idea forward as a team — the pipeline supports both individual and collaborative submissions.

partnersSection.title: Our Partners
partnersSection.subtitle: We collaborate with entities across government, academia, and the private sector.

contact.title: Have a question?
contact.body: Reach out to the Innovation Office — we're happy to help you shape an idea, understand the pipeline, or explore collaboration.

sections.title: About the Authority
sections.body: The General Authority for Competition (GAC) protects and promotes fair competition in the Saudi market, combating monopolistic practices and enabling a healthy business environment aligned with Vision 2030.

sections.title: About the Program
sections.body: The Innovation to Impact program is a structured pipeline that captures ideas from employees, entrepreneurs, and partners and guides them across eight stages — from strategic framing to measured impact.

sections.title: Objectives
sections.body: Build a culture of innovation, shorten the path from idea to implementation, and ensure every accepted idea is linked to a measurable outcome that serves the Authority’s mandate.

sections.title: Strategic Alignment
sections.body: Every idea is evaluated against the Authority’s strategic themes and national priorities, ensuring innovation effort is focused where it creates the most value.

targetAudience.title: Who Can Participate
targetAudience.subtitle: The program welcomes innovators from across the ecosystem.

cards.title: Entrepreneurs & Startups
cards.body: Founders and emerging companies with market-ready solutions relevant to competition, market monitoring, and consumer protection.

cards.title: Government Entities
cards.body: Public-sector teams seeking to co-develop regulatory and analytical tools, and to pilot cross-agency initiatives.

cards.title: Innovation Specialists
cards.body: Researchers, data scientists, and domain experts who can strengthen ideas with evidence, methods, and technical depth.

evaluationCriteria.title: Evaluation Criteria
evaluationCriteria.subtitle: How submitted ideas are assessed by evaluators and the committee.

criteria.name: Strategic Alignment
criteria.desc: How closely the idea supports the Authority’s strategic themes and national priorities.

criteria.name: Innovation
criteria.desc: Originality and the degree to which the idea improves on existing approaches.

criteria.name: Feasibility
criteria.desc: Technical and operational practicality within available resources and timeframe.

criteria.name: Impact
criteria.desc: Expected scale and significance of the outcome if implemented.

criteria.name: Effort
criteria.desc: Cost, complexity, and effort required relative to the expected benefit.

evaluationCriteria.note: Each criterion is scored from 1 to 5. The weighted total determines whether an idea advances, requires revision, or is escalated to the committee.

expectedSolutions.title: Solutions We Are Looking For
expectedSolutions.subtitle: Examples of the kinds of solutions the program seeks across priority areas.

items: Data platforms to detect anti-competitive behavior

items: AI for document and case analysis

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to common questions about the program.

items.a: Employees of the Authority, entrepreneurs, government entities, and innovation specialists are all welcome to submit.
Initial screening happens within a few working days; full technical evaluation typically completes within two to three weeks.
Yes. Ideas are handled according to the confidentiality level and reviewed only by authorized evaluators and committee members.

items.q: Can I submit more than one idea?
items.a: Yes, there is no limit on the number of ideas you may submit.

items.q: What happens if my idea needs changes?
items.a: You will receive structured feedback and can submit a revised version linked to your original idea.

items.q: Do I retain ownership of my idea?
items.a: Ownership terms are described in the IP terms you acknowledge when submitting.

items.q: Will I be notified of progress?
items.a: Yes. You receive in-app notifications at each key lifecycle event.
Ideas are scored on strategic alignment, innovation, feasibility, impact, and effort. See the Evaluation Criteria page.
You can reach the innovation team via the Support page for guidance at any stage.
Submission windows are announced on the Roadmap page and reflected in the countdown on the home page.

support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Get in touch with the innovation team.
support.name: Your name
support.subject: Subject
support.sent: Thank you — your message has been recorded. The team will reach out shortly.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
body: This Privacy Policy explains how the General Authority for Competition collects, uses, and protects personal information submitted through the Innovation to Impact platform.
We collect only the information necessary to receive, evaluate, and act on submitted ideas, including your name, contact details, and the content of your submission.
Your information is accessible only to authorized personnel involved in evaluation and program administration, and is never sold or shared for marketing purposes.
We retain submission data for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described here and to comply with applicable regulations.
You may request access to or correction of your personal information by contacting the innovation team through the Support page.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
body: By using the Innovation to Impact platform you agree to these Terms & Conditions.
You are responsible for the accuracy of the information you submit and for ensuring you have the right to share it.
Submitted ideas are subject to the intellectual-property terms acknowledged at submission time.
The Authority may accept, request revision of, reject, or escalate any submission at its discretion based on the published evaluation criteria.
The platform is provided on an "as is" basis; the Authority may update these terms and program details from time to time.
